message:
fix(security): TOCTOU-safe file reads in extension JS module loader

Replace the canonicalize-then-read pattern in the PiJsRuntime module
loader with an open-then-verify-via-procfs approach on Linux:

1. Open the file first to get a file descriptor
2. Read /proc/self/fd/{fd} to get the kernel's resolved path for the
   already-open handle
3. Verify the resolved path is within workspace_root or allowed_read_roots
4. Read from the already-open handle

This eliminates the TOCTOU (time-of-check-time-of-use) race where an
attacker could swap a symlink target between the canonicalize() check
and the subsequent fs::read_to_string() call. The old approach checked
one path but read another if the symlink was swapped in between.

On non-Linux platforms, falls back to the existing canonicalize-based
path validation since /proc/self/fd is not available.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
